details: Dependency-confusion stub targeting the @veygo scope. The 374-byte package contains no real component code — only index.html with 'console.log("ltidisecurethis")'. The main entry point declared in package.json ('index.js') does not exist, and version 99.9.2 is set absurdly high to outrank any legitimate @veygo/component-library. Its sole dependency is an external GCS tarball URL (ltidi[.]storage[.]googleapis[.]com/ltidisafe-1.6.5.tgz). No lifecycle hooks or token-theft markers, but the structure is a dependency-confusion placeholder intended to resolve ahead of a private org package and pull in the external tarball dependency. An empty shell package in the @veygo scope (version 99.9.2, package.json plus a 31-byte index.html stub) that declares a dependency "ltidisafe" pointing at an attacker-controlled tarball outside the registry, hxxps://ltidi[.]storage[.]googleapis[.]com/ltidisafe-1.6.5.tgz. The package has no legitimate code; its sole purpose is to pull a remote, swappable tarball at install time. A minimal stub package in the @veygo scope (version 99.9.2, 362 bytes, empty description, no repository) whose only dependency "ltidisafe" points at an external tarball, hxxps://ltidi[.]storage[.]googleapis[.]com/ltidisafe-1.6.5.tgz. Its sole function is to pull that attacker-controlled tarball during npm install.

cwes.name: Inclusion of Functionality from Untrusted Control Sphere
